title: The Lone Hacker That Found NASA’s Secret UFO Fleet [Gary McKinnon Interview]
summary: Gary McKinnon, a self-taught British hacker, describes how he broke into NASA, Pentagon, and military networks in 2000-2001 searching for UFO evidence. He claims to have seen a photograph of a cigar-shaped craft above Earth at NASA's Johnson Space Center Building 8, and found a spreadsheet titled 'non-terrestrial officers,' before being disconnected mid-session. The interview covers his hacking methods, findings, and the subsequent 10-year extradition battle with the US government.

key_claims.claim: McKinnon found a photograph at NASA Johnson Space Center Building 8 showing a cigar-shaped silvery craft above Earth with geodesic domes on top and bottom
McKinnon found a spreadsheet titled 'non-terrestrial officers' containing names and ranks
US military and NASA networks had blank passwords or simple passwords like 'password' or 'admin' in 2000
Donna Hare, a NASA photographic specialist, claimed her colleague showed her satellite photos of UFOs that were airbrushed out before public release
McKinnon had no involvement with Solar Warden and it originated from an anonymous post on Above Top Secret forum
US authorities wanted McKinnon to serve 70 years in prison for the hacking
Ed Gibson, US embassy attaché in London, said 'We want to see him fry' referring to McKinnon
McKinnon was on Interpol's red list and cannot travel to the US
McKinnon hacked into approximately 97 US military and government sites
The craft McKinnon saw appeared smooth with no visible rivets, seams, or antenna
